# UC-22.50.25 · Basel III OPR25.8 — Business continuity and resilience: recovery objective monitoring

## Description

Monitors BCP/DR test outcomes against RTO/RPO targets and flags critical services that have not been tested within 180 days, providing evidence of operational resilience per OPR25.8.

## Value

OPR25.8 requires banks to maintain business continuity plans with defined recovery objectives. Continuous monitoring of test results and recovery metrics provides auditable evidence that resilience controls are effective.

## Implementation

(1) Ingest BCP/DR test results with target_rto, target_rpo, actual_recovery_time, and data_loss_minutes; (2) schedule monthly; (3) flag services failing targets or overdue for testing. Indexes required: `index=bcp_dr`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=backup_verification`, `sourcetype=bcp_test_results`, `sourcetype=dr_failover`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: BCP/DR test results, failover event logs, backup verification records, critical-service availability metrics.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Ingest BCP/DR test results with target_rto, target_rpo, actual_recovery_time, and data_loss_minutes; (2) schedule monthly; (3) flag services failing targets or overdue for testing.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=bcp_dr sourcetype IN (bcp_test_results, dr_failover, backup_verification) earliest=-90d
| eval rto_met=if(actual_recovery_time<=target_rto, 1, 0)
| eval rpo_met=if(data_loss_minutes<=target_rpo, 1, 0)
| stats count AS tests sum(rto_met) AS rto_pass sum(rpo_met) AS rpo_pass latest(test_date) AS last_test BY critical_service, test_type
| eval rto_compliance_pct=round(100*rto_pass/tests, 1)
| eval rpo_compliance_pct=round(100*rpo_pass/tests, 1)
| eval days_since_test=round((now()-strptime(last_test, "%Y-%m-%d"))/86400)
| where rto_compliance_pct<100 OR rpo_compliance_pct<100 OR days_since_test>180
| table critical_service, test_type, tests, rto_compliance_pct, rpo_compliance_pct, days_since_test
```

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the GRC system and the audit register, and with the crosswalk for the tier-2 framework clause, so monitoring evidence matches the mapped control.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table of flagged records for investigation; single-value KPI of coverage / compliance percentage; time chart of trend over the last 90 days; drill-down per responsible owner.

## SPL

```spl
index=bcp_dr sourcetype IN (bcp_test_results, dr_failover, backup_verification) earliest=-90d
| eval rto_met=if(actual_recovery_time<=target_rto, 1, 0)
| eval rpo_met=if(data_loss_minutes<=target_rpo, 1, 0)
| stats count AS tests sum(rto_met) AS rto_pass sum(rpo_met) AS rpo_pass latest(test_date) AS last_test BY critical_service, test_type
| eval rto_compliance_pct=round(100*rto_pass/tests, 1)
| eval rpo_compliance_pct=round(100*rpo_pass/tests, 1)
| eval days_since_test=round((now()-strptime(last_test, "%Y-%m-%d"))/86400)
| where rto_compliance_pct<100 OR rpo_compliance_pct<100 OR days_since_test>180
| table critical_service, test_type, tests, rto_compliance_pct, rpo_compliance_pct, days_since_test
```

## Visualization

Table of flagged records for investigation; single-value KPI of coverage / compliance percentage; time chart of trend over the last 90 days; drill-down per responsible owner. Key fields: `critical_service`, `days_since_test`, `last_test`, `rpo_compliance_pct`, `rpo_met`, `rpo_pass`.
